name: John Philip Sousa
abstract: John Philip Sousa (November 6, 1854 – March 6, 1932) was an American composer and conductor of the late Romantic era known primarily for US military marches. He is known as "The March King" (or the "American March King" to distinguish him from his British counterpart Kenneth J.

concerts.label: New York Symphony Orchestra/ Sousa's Military Band

works.t: La damnation de Faust, Op. 24: Rákóczy March
La damnation de Faust, Op. 24: Reveille
La damnation de Faust, Op. 24: Easter Hymn
Three Quotations: 1. The King of France

works_created.t: I've Made My Plans for the Summer
The Caissons Go Rolling Along (U.S. Field Artillery March)

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: Guess where Scott Joplin stole that from? John Philip Sousa’s marches. It’s exactly the same form. He just put his syncopated melodies and jazz harmonies on top of that march form which John Philip Sousa started to create in the 1870s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Nick Brignola) states that his grandfather played with the Sousa band, not himself. There is no evidence of a direct personal relationship between Nick Brignola and John Phillip Sousa.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: I never took any lessons as a kid, I never learned to double tongue or triple tongue. I had no classical training whatsoever you know, other than playing marches in my high school band, that was it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Israels
about_quotes.evidence: I’m talking about the period starting with Scott Joplin and Jelly Roll Morton and John Philip Sousa, that’s the late nineteenth century
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: For example, in John Phillip Sousa’s band, the trumpet players and the cornet players – this is where the melody was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie B. Edwards
about_quotes.evidence: I'm reminded of this uh Helmecki who was a very famous bass drummer around he played with uh John Philip Sousa
